title: Pair 01: Dessert Lounge vs Family Trattoria
summary: This comparison keeps the queue logic, booking protection, and service rules the same, but swaps the dining-room footprint. Option A uses a dessert lounge built around couples and small groups, while Option B uses a family trattoria with more large tables to test how the same large-party wave feels in two very different rooms.

A.label: Dessert lounge layout
A.notes: The room favors couples and date-night traffic, so larger bookings create seat-fit tension under the same arrival wave.

B.label: Family trattoria layout
B.notes: The floor dedicates more six- and eight-seat tables to absorb celebration parties with less reshuffling.
